title: Pre-endpoint fail-closed safeguards FH-1/FH-2/FH-3 (impossible-date, same-applicability overlap, guard container)
objective: Smallest fail-closed safeguard task; additive; no lock/manifest/contract change; nothing Published/Verified. FH-1: services/api/app/rules/evaluator.py _valid_iso_date must reject IMPOSSIBLE calendar dates (e.g. 2024-02-30, 2024-04-31) via true datetime.date(y,m,d) validation in try/except ValueError, so a malformed as_of_date fails closed to professional_review_required (not lexically sorted as if valid). FH-3: services/api/app/rules/integration.py assert_not_verified must not raise TypeError on a foreign payload whose 'evaluations' (or family_coverage) is a non-list; coerce via an isinstance/list guard so a hostile/foreign payload fails safe. FH-2 (CONSERVATIVE, fail-closed ONLY; NOT a rule_series redesign): guard the same-applicability overlapping-effective-window case so that if more than one in-effect rule in a family would BOTH apply to the same inputs over overlapping windows, the result surfaces professional_review_required / a typed conflict rather than silently picking or combining — the full rule_series grouping remains explicit future work. This must NOT wrongly reject legitimate DIFFERENT-applicability multi-district families (the G4-L1 constraint). Add deterministic tests for each: impossible-date fail-closed; non-list evaluations fails safe; same-applicability overlap surfaces review while different-applicability family still evaluates. Scope: evaluator.py, registry.py, integration.py, and their tests under services/api/tests/rules/**. Does NOT modify lock files, dependency tooling, contracts, M4-T001/002/003 accepted behavior beyond these additive guards, or any legal RULE CONTENT.
business_reason: Recorded future-hardening items FH-1/FH-2/FH-3 (project-control/reports/M4-RULES-FUTURE-HARDENING.md) are prerequisites the owner requires BEFORE the rules-evaluation result is exposed through any public property-analysis endpoint/UI. Each is a fail-closed safeguard against an untrusted or ambiguous input reaching the evaluator; today they are safe only because callers are trusted in-process. Owner directive 2026-07-22: address FH-1/FH-2/FH-3 in the smallest properly controlled task before endpoint exposure.
inputs: services/api/tests/rules/** (existing test conventions incl. test_rules_engine_hardening.py, test_rules_integration.py)
outputs: Additive fail-closed guards for FH-1/FH-2/FH-3 in evaluator.py/registry.py/integration.py
requirement_refs: CLAUDE.md permanent principle #1 (qualified humans approve legal interpretations — FH-2 must fail closed, never make a legal pick)
forbidden_paths: any rule JSON / legal rule content
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 (FH-1 primary): as_of_date='2024-02-30' (well-formed but impossible) -> professional_review_required fail-closed (in_effect False); a real date '2024-02-29' (leap) still valid.

AS-3 (FH-3): assert_not_verified given a payload whose 'evaluations' is a non-list (e.g. an int) does NOT raise TypeError and still fails closed on any verified status; a non-list family_coverage likewise guarded.

AS-4 (FH-2 fail-closed): a synthetic family with TWO in-effect rules whose applicability BOTH match the same inputs over overlapping windows -> surfaces professional_review_required / typed conflict (no silent pick or combine).

AS-5 (FH-2 no false-positive / G4-L1): a family with two in-effect rules of DIFFERENT applicability (e.g. distinct districts) still evaluates normally for inputs matching exactly one -> unchanged behavior, NOT rejected.
AS-6 (regression): full services/api suite green; strict-JSON / provenance / uncertainty / never-Verified invariants preserved; no rule Published/Verified.

progress_log.message: G0 PASS; claimed; scope FH-1/FH-2/FH-3 fail-closed safeguards; awaiting owner confirm on FH-2 conservative-guard scope before implementation
